name: CVXPY (Convex Optimization)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); Stanford; free for academic, commercial use

auth.notes: Local library; commercial solvers (Gurobi, MOSEK) require separate license

performance.notes: Problem-dependent; LP/QP in milliseconds; large SDP can take minutes

agent_readiness.notes: Domain-specific language for convex optimization. Express optimization problems naturally in Python (minimize/maximize expressions subject to constraints). Automatic convexity verification (DCP rules). Multiple solver backends: SCS, ECOS, CLARABEL (default), Gurobi, MOSEK, CPLEX. LP, QP, SOCP, SDP, MIP. Used in ML (LASSO, SVM training), control systems, portfolio optimization, resource allocation, signal processing.

gotchas: DCP rules can be confusing — `violation of DCP rules` error common
Open-source solvers slower than commercial (Gurobi/MOSEK) for large problems
Problem size limitations for SDP without commercial solvers
summary: CVXPY is a Python DSL for convex optimization. Express LP, QP, SOCP, SDP problems naturally — automatic convexity verification via DCP rules. Multiple solver backends (SCS, ECOS, Gurobi, MOSEK). Used for portfolio optimization, control systems, ML with regularization, and resource allocation. Excellent for convex problems; requires reformulation for nonconvex.
